file_insights.changes_summary: Added cleanup infrastructure: dir_size() helper, CleanupStats struct, perform_cleanup() function
key_additions: dir_size(path: &Path) -> u64 - recursive directory size calculation
CleanupStats struct with 5 tracking fields (corrupt_cards_removed, old_failed_cards_removed, orphan_running_cards_removed, target_dirs_removed, bytes_freed)
perform_cleanup(scan_result: &ScanResult, dry_run: bool) - removal orchestrator with dry-run support

patterns_discovered.name: Dry-Run Branching
patterns_discovered.description: Conditional println!() for dry-run vs actual fs::remove_dir_all() removal

patterns_discovered.name: Error Context Propagation
patterns_discovered.description: Use of .with_context() for detailed error messages including path display

patterns_discovered.name: Accumulator Stats Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: CleanupStats struct accumulates counts and bytes_freed across all removal categories
patterns_discovered.impact: Single return value tracking all metrics

patterns_discovered.name: Recursive Directory Traversal
patterns_discovered.description: dir_size() recursively walks directory tree to calculate total size
patterns_discovered.impact: Accurate byte accounting for removal operations
patterns_discovered.optimization_note: Called before removal in both dry-run and real paths

gotchas_discovered.description: fs::metadata() errors are silently dropped via .flatten()
gotchas_discovered.risk: Permission errors or missing files could silently reduce reported bytes_freed accuracy

gotchas_discovered.description: Four near-identical removal blocks: corrupt, old_failed, orphan_running, target_dirs
gotchas_discovered.risk: Maintenance burden; bugs in one block could be copied to others

gotchas_discovered.description: dir_size() is called separately from removal, requiring two passes for each item
gotchas_discovered.risk: Performance impact on large trees; files could change between traversals in concurrent scenarios
gotchas_discovered.optimization_note: Not critical for card cleanup but worth noting

gotchas_discovered.description: target/ cleanup using fs::remove_dir_all() - memory from MEMORY.md shows this caused ENOENT in moved workspaces
gotchas_discovered.risk: Cargo incremental artifacts in moved directories could still fail if not cleaned properly

approach_outcome.completion: Full removal logic implemented with dry-run support
scope: Orphan running card removal (missing process check)
target/ directory cleanup (addresses stale cargo artifacts)
deliverables: dir_size() helper for accurate byte accounting
[DRY-RUN] prefixed output for safe preview mode
approach_outcome.integration_readiness: Function signature matches ScanResult from previous subtask-1-2; ready for CLI command binding

recommendations.action: Add unit tests for dry-run mode ensuring no files are actually deleted
Extract repeated removal pattern into closure or generic helper function
recommendations.rationale: Reduce duplication in corrupt/old_failed/orphan/target removal blocks
recommendations.example: fn remove_items<T: AsRef<Path>>(items: &[T], label: &str, dry_run: bool, stats: &mut CleanupStats) -> Result<()>

recommendations.action: Add warning logging for silently skipped fs::metadata() errors in dir_size()
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation may mask permission or I/O issues
recommendations.approach: Log skipped entries; optionally add strict mode flag

recommendations.action: Verify target/ cleanup works correctly with jj workspace moves
recommendations.rationale: MEMORY.md notes ENOENT issues when cargo creates subdirs in moved trees
recommendations.validation: Test cleanup after 'bop move' or 'jj workspace' operations

recommendations.action: Document stats fields in CleanupStats struct
recommendations.rationale: Clarify meaning of each count for future maintainers
